mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.5pp, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in over 60% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate supply chain bottlenecks and inventory shortages that force corporate margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns do not decline following INDPRO contractions, or the mechanism shows zero predictive power during periods of confirmed supply chain disruptions.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 4.0% year-over-year, inflation expectations detach from central bank targets, forcing markets to price in aggressive rate hikes that suppress equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets generate positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 4.0% threshold, or the signal Sharpe ratio remains negative across all backtest periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate consumer spending capacity and reduces corporate wage pressure per unit of output, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no positive correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 10-year sample, or the Rank IC falls below 0.01 in rolling window evaluations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0% signals an economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings revisions, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following month as investors discount future cash flows.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.0%, or the signal fails to predict direction during confirmed recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates labor market inefficiency and rising unit labor costs that compress profit margins, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the interaction effect loses predictive significance when tested against a baseline single-factor model.
